name: Apple Music
philosophy: An inspired interpretation of Apple Music's marketing design language — a light-canvas streaming service that pairs Apple's clean SF Pro typography with full-bleed album-cover-driven multi-color hero bands. The brand surface is a white canvas chrome with the album-cover artwork acting as the entire chromatic system — every page derives its color from the featured album/artist, making the brand a chameleon dressed in SF Pro.

design_language.motion_signature: Signature is the album-cover parallax — featured artwork shifts at 30% of scroll velocity creating a depth effect; play-button hover scales to 1.05 at 200ms ease-out; the player floats up at 400ms cubic-bezier on first paint.

trademark_signals: Album-cover-as-hero — every featured page leads with a giant full-bleed album cover (frequently 800–1200px tall) — the artwork IS the chromatic system
Apple Music red (#fa233b) for the brand mark and 'Try free' CTAs — the one fixed accent in an otherwise dynamic-color system
SF Pro Display weight 700 for hero album titles and weight 600 for artist names — the brand's typographic signature
Floating play-button — a circular red-fill button with white triangle, appearing on hover over album/track tiles — the brand's most-recognized interaction
Editorial 'For You' grid — large 2-up featured tiles + 4-up secondary + horizontal-scroll rails of playlist cards
ai_slop_avoided: Don't paint a brand-color background. The album cover IS the chromatic system; the canvas stays white between covers
Don't drop the red (#fa233b) for the brand mark. The Apple Music red is the only fixed accent
Don't substitute SF Pro with Helvetica. The slight optical refinements of SF Pro are non-negotiable on Apple surfaces
Don't shrink album covers below 400px on hero. The brand surfaces the artwork at scale
Don't add gradient overlays on album covers. The artwork is presented as-is
